item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am pretty sure the child is a manequin, otherwise it would have to eb a chidl that went to hell. Also Ice Cream Hell. NOICE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frickin' GOLD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually thought this was a hell customized for everyone that entered (The agent had issues with weight in his youth so his hell revolved around disgusting ice cream) and that concept actually seemed more interesting. But ice cream hell is cool too. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(To the tune of "Robot Hell" from Futurama)

For wrecking teeth, there'll be a reckoning
For all your sins of fudge and caramel
Now you melt and boil
Mixed with glass and engine oil
Here on level one of Ice Cream Hell!

+ show block
– hide block

Making children fat and lazy
Suducing them with sugar-laden goo
For your crimes you will atone
In Baskin' heat or Coldest Stone
The Humor's Good on Level Two!

THIS SCOOP MUST SUFFER!

"This cannot be!"
Check out these grim nutrition facts!
"There must be desserts worse than me!"
We've shopped around, there really aren't

"Then please let me explain:
My taste brings people such delight"
You cause headaches with every bite!
"Hey, don't blame me! Blame Ben & Jerry!"
You dishoner all things dairy!

Causing cramps and diarrhea
Lactose intolerance is not a crime!
Hey, waffle cone in my hand
Tell me how you taste when you're tastin' sand!
That's what-that's what-that's what you get,
on Level Five!

This is intense…
"Keep going Agent, stay alert"
Why Ice Cream Hell? It makes no sense!
It's not like ice cream can get hurt
The implications are immense
Although, I must admit
I kinda understand them a little bit
"Don't dawdle, agent! Don't delay!"
The sins of ice cream must be paid…

Death by Chocolate, French vanilla
Rum Raisin and Cotton Candy too
No flavor will be saved
Far from anyone who craved
your taste with which you have enslaved
And now your fate is most depraved:
Trapped forever here in ICE CREAM HEEEEEEELL!!!


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Agreed with MacLeod and you. I've edited the WikiWalk post accordingly.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Concurring with sirpudding and MacLeod. The 2915 crosslink is the most reasonable one here, connecting two articles with seemingly frozen pits to hell.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
In my headcanon,

I feel like wikiwalk should be about word-of-God, author-sanctioned/author-canon connections, or connections that don't interfere with that. Pop culture's idea of demons torturing ice cream for all time doesn't seem to jive with a being that has no problem forcing others to mutilate children or with a cult whose end goal is to eat God. I'm with SP here and saying this might be a bad move.

In short, the connection you're proposing is reliant on your headcanon and a bit contrived. I like the Frostee Flesh crosslink idea better.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Sarkicism is a blend of gnosticism and Lovecraftian themes, and I imagine there isn't a "hell" at all, save the ones that we make for ourselves (which of course we do over and over again).

The Scarlet King is Satan as Elder God, and I think that while there is certainly a hell there, it isn't one with goofy industrious blue-collar imps and punishment for naughty ice cream. Instead it is literally unimaginable and utterly inhuman horror: like 110 Montauk as a place that is also eternal.

I don't think that it makes sense to link this article to either mythos, it just isn't compatible.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I apologize if I'm not conveying this well enough, let me explain further:

+ show block
– hide block

In my headcanon, I imagine that there are multiple hells. There's a human hell, there's a giraffe hell, there's a penguin hell, there's a hell specifically for hats, and a hell for meteors. Of course, there's a hell for ice cream. These hells all coexist without conflict. Richard and Sons' main business then, in my opinion, is opening up gateways to these hells, for various reasons. One of the best things about this skip is that you never really know what SCP-2731 is for. The sloped floors imply that it's being used to drain something in, or get rid of something. However, the sloping of the floors could also be to, theoretically, keep something out. It could also, just as easily, be something that the store uses to get supplies. Or some other, bizarre reason we can;t even comprehend. It's a fun thought to entertain, what exactly it's for, and it's one of the things this SCP does best.

Getting back on topic, there are possibly other people or groups that want other various hells opened up for them, for their various reasons. Sarkics could want the hell where Sarkic critters go, or the hell where they could easily dispose of a sinful Mekhanite, or whatever. Likewise, the Scarlett King could also have their own uses for this hell-opening service.

As for the "too frivolous" complaint, I partially agree with you there. However, consider the following: you have this frivolous, hell-opening service that also possibly services other, more sinister anomalous groups. Juxtaposing the frivolous tone of this SCP with the clinical tone of the others would do good, in my opinion.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I don't think that an idea as frivolous and goofy as "hell for ice cream" is especially compatible with either Sarkicism or Scarlet King. I'd be very surprised if either Metaphysician or Clef respectively would disagree with this.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
My rationale here is that I was really thinking of "these guys are misinterpreting Sarkicism/Scarlett King" so they thought it was Satanism. Also, the workers said that they were building "demonholes", and maybe the store itself requested a demonhole with specifically ice cream in it?

Also, SCP-2336 gives me pause because it's pretty general and you probably wouldn't need a demonhole to access it, and SCP-2915 is pretty short and vague, and the page says it's in Ohio, while SCP-2731 says the Satanist group is in North Carolina.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Sarkicism doesn't really deal with conventional Goetia like this (there are like regular imps here, not weird Lovecraftian flesh monsters molded from ancient Daeva or whatever) and this is a bit light hearted for Scarlet King mythos.

This seems more similar to the kind of magic in SCP-2336.

This is also very similar to SCP-2915 which also has a retail establishment using a gate to hell.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-2915 fits nicely in this SCP. This SCP mentions a "Satanists Club", which could just as easily be the force responsible for creating SCP-2915, and both this SCP and SCP-2915 involve frozen pits leading to hell.

Editing this:

Operative B: I think it's a Satanists club or something out in Hendersonville.

To this:

Operative B: I think it's a Satanists club or something out in Hendersonville.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"ice creamons"
Out-fucking-standing. That's the exact moment I knew I was going to upvote this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see that the Latin in this skip's been discussed a lot here. I haven't studied Latin for a while now, so my comments here may be rather inaccurate, but I'd like to offer my own interpretation of the translations.

"Dulcis casus ad inferna" - 'ad' is normally followed by the accusative, but 'inferna' seems to be nominative. Although Latin doesn't appear to have a direct parallel to the biblical hell, it seems that 'Gehenna' is used in Latin bibles. I would recommend the translation "Descendum ad Gehennam dulce est".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the king of free association SCPs. I don't know how Silber managed to get "hell" and "ice cream" together in a coherent and believable way, but man does it work. And it is unsettling, for all the reasons mentioned.

This SCP is inspirational as hell from a writing standpoint.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This creeped me out. It's hell for ice cream? And there's a company dedicated to maintaining the entrance to ice cream hell? Why? And for what reason? What are they collecting from this ice cream hell?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I went ahead and slotted these in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally came back to this and changed up the "1000 hours in Google Translate" Latin phrases.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ice cream Hell. I aspire to have ideas this insane. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pardon the necropost but please don't do it, think of Moth Helm!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this for a large number of reasons.

1. I'm a sucker for insane, surreal things done on an epic scale, especially if it's sledgehammer-to-crack-a-walnut. I love this example even more because the motivations behind it aren't completely inscrutable — you can just about imagine some demented worldview in which ice cream hell is a thing that should exist.

2. Amazing imagery. Utterly wonderful. And the imagery is paired with a fantastic level of detail to paint that hell around me in 4D technicolour.

3. Realistic, faintly ominous dialogue that's also genuinely funny.

4. You've left just the right amount of mystery to allow the reader to ponder the whys and hows of the scip without feeling dissatisfied.

One of my favourite articles on the site, hands down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1'd, as this article is a fine example of fucking weird done well.

item.title: Dulce descensus Averni
item.content:encoded:
The translation of this is really iffy. There is no Latin word for hell, but the Avernus in Campainia was believed by some to be the ENTERANCE to hell. There is no way that Averni means hell.

descensus hello google translate. This is technically a PPP (perfect passive participle) so this literally means "having been gone down on" which makes no sense.

Via ad Tartara dulce est.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Corrections to the Latin:

"Dulce descensus Averni" -> "Dulcis casus ad inferna" (lit. Sweet [is] the fall to hell)
"Demolitus dentium" -> "Destructores dentium" (lit. Destroyers of teeth)
"Saginare quisquilia" -> "Purgamenta crassificantia" (lit. Fattening garbage)
"Impensus saccharum" -> "Tumidae saccharis" (lit. Bloated with/from sugar)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I say Hat Hell, a place where sinful headgear must go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ice Cream Hell. Check.
Giraffe Hell. Check.

What comes next?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the concept of a separate Hell existing just for ice cream products, but I don't get what the first part - the Richard and Sons thing - has to do with it. Seems like that part could be taken out entirely and the article would still read the same.

Again though, it's ice cream hell, and that deserves my upvote. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A adore a good dose of WTF on my friday morning. Good job. Raises a small question in my headspace though. Is the ice cream in hell, or are human souls made of ice cream?

…either way +1

item.title: I really like the idea that ice cream is sentient and capable of sin.
item.content:encoded:
Also, I feel like this one is different enough from scp-1557 to set itself apart.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess you're right. Still not that happy about it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I disagree with you here. I feel like the characters could be saying this to each other without it serving only as exposition if Operative B was new to the business. I feel like this is the case because they remind him that he used to work at Domino's.

Everything just screams "Operative B is a new guy" to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I scream "upvote", you scream "upvote", we all scream "upvote" for ice cream upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell Hell Hell - Shop for Hell Hell Hell Online

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ice cream hell.
Let that sink in.
A.
Hell.
For.
Ice.
Cream.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy… I was going to include a portal related GoI in my first SCP attempt, can I borrow this one? Their focus on the local market might even serve as a greatest justification of what I was about to do with my idea.

Nonetheless, I scream "Upvote"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hell Hell?

Followed by Hell Hell Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Salty caramel maybe!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the bizzareness of Ice Cream Hell, but honestly I found it a tad too reminiscent of Giraffe Hell for me to upvote. Also, this falls into the pitfall of "over-explainy dialogue", where the characters say things to each other for the sole purpose of exposition for the reader when they'd otherwise not say it. Biggest offender in my opinion was this line,

It means greater got-dang Asheville. We're a local business, we're family-run, I'm a third-generation manager. We provide a niche service to a very specific area. Are you going to pay your own goddamn gas money and van maintenance to head out into the fucking sticks to open a demonhole at some redneck's cabin?

Like, I can't imagine these people saying this for any other reason than to explain to the reader that this business is family-run, local and that the speaker is a manager.
Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand where you're coming from. The "otherworldly entity who talks like an average internet user for the sake of amusing bathos" trope is getting played out.

However, I don't think it's applicable here. Just because these guys deal with otherworldly entities doesn't mean that they think like them. They might be humans caught up in something much weirder than they are, or they might be entities with the appearance and thoughts of humans; that's for the reader to decide. Either way, the intent here actually was to write an average, somewhat curmudgeonly repairman and a younger guy who may or may not be his son who work for a small service-and-repair business in the South somewhere, so I'm pleased to hear someone vouch for the accuracy there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's actually pretty realistic dialogue, even for a company that deals in creating gateways to Ice Cream Hell.

This is a subset of a thing where authors on the site assume that extraworldly entities, many of which aren't even human, talk just like the author does in real life. "It sounds just like a twenty-something white American male on the internet" does not equal "realistic dialogue."

Unless all those twelve-year-olds in the chat are right and the Foundation is real, you and your brother do not, in fact, work for a company that opens portals to Ice Cream Hell. So while the dialogue makes absolute sense for two guys working for a plumbing company, I'm deeply skeptical that it would be exactly the same thing for two guys working for Richard and Son.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As far as the employees' dialogue is concerned, I work for my parents' small plumbing company, and my brother, who's also my supervisor, and I have that exact same conversation all the damn time. It's actually pretty realistic dialogue, even for a company that deals in creating gateways to Ice Cream Hell.

Also, You scream, I scream, we all scream for ice cream!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This could have very easily been too silly to survive, and for some it may violate that particular threshold. The way in which this was executed, though, allows for several concepts that are actually quite serious in their implications. This is one of those articles that becomes better the more one reflects on it as a result.

For instance, what exactly is the nature of the extradimensional space in which Ice Cream Hell is located? Is it something that is real in a scientifically understandable sense (insofar as science can grasp at any given time the concept of existence outside of the traditional 4-D model)? Or given its resemblance to a traditional Western vision of Hell, is it some sort of belief-based construct affecting humans?

And what kind of person would order something like that being installed in their store? Can you imagine the kind of person who opens a grocery store and then decides that they need a place of eternal punishment for their dairy products?

The only thing that really struck me as off was the first addendum. I think I like the idea of Richard and Sons being faceless a little better, as it adds to the mystery of this phenomena. The two technicians were a little goofy (especially the bit about them hiring people from Domino's to open hell-portals), and the fact that they are still extant and reachable by phone brings up the unresolved issue of why the Foundation can't track these guys. Not bothersome enough to affect my vote, however.

My favorite part though, is how there are (to me anyway) two major interpretations of what's going on in Ice Cream Hell. You either have this group of demons in this crazy hellscape bent on causing suffering to inanimate lumps of ice cream, which is absurdly hilarious as one imagines the lengths these beings are going to in order to uselessly torment something. Or, on the other hand, ice cream is somehow sentient and capable of sin. And then it goes to Hell if it's not good at being ice cream. Which, you know, huh.

Upvoted. "Fucking weird" is risky to undertake, but sometimes it hits something very satisfying in the reader's mind, and this seems to have done the trick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this hell for ice cream? As in, the place where ice cream goes when it dies to be eternally punished? Am I correct in this assumption? Because if so, what?

While I can't say it's not a brilliant execution of the concept, the concept itself is, well… Ice Cream Hell. I realize SCPs have been getting progressively sillier, but good grief! This makes Giraffe Hell look sour-faced and serious by comparison.

That being said, I'm still upvoting this for being an enjoyable, original, well-crafted piece of fiction, which is really all I ask for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I swear this is the most original concept in months. I'm salty that I didn't come up with it first.

So well done, Silber. So good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good. Well done, Silber. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delicious sin. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Best concept.

Can we consider Chair Hell. Imagine, people with no butts.

Elbow hell?

Hell Hell?

cryogen get in here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just read this in chat not 20 minutes ago and loved it! +1
